title: Dangerous Curves

jokes.text: Where were you when you first heard this? I'll tell you where I was. I was out eating... I think I had kung pao... and I ended up on the... I can't listen to this anymore.
jokes.setup: Radio DJ asking where listeners were during a 'historic' sound effect
jokes.punchline: His own rambling story becomes so boring he cuts himself off
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons meta-humor about boring radio content, with good self-awareness

jokes.text: Nobody shuts out my blather!
jokes.setup: Homer turning off boring radio show
jokes.punchline: Radio DJ's indignant response using the word 'blather' which admits his content is meaningless
jokes.explanation: Efficient wordplay that's self-aware about radio quality

jokes.text: They don't look like the stabby kind.
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to pick up hitchhikers
jokes.punchline: Her casual assessment that they're not the 'stabby kind'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge logic - overly optimistic with casual violence reference. Great economy.

jokes.text: I said they were thrill-crazed drug-seekers. Don't put words in my mouth.
jokes.setup: Child pointing out Marge's contradiction about hitchhikers
jokes.punchline: Marge's pedantic correction that completely misses the point
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's tendency to make meaningless distinctions

jokes.text: Were we ever that stupid? Of course we were.
jokes.setup: Homer asking rhetorical question about young love
jokes.punchline: Marge's immediate, matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good timing, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: It's called 'biking out.' It's a combination of biking and making out.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to have invented something new
jokes.punchline: The obvious portmanteau and his pride in this 'invention'
jokes.explanation: Classic young Homer stupidity - thinking he invented something obvious

jokes.text: Hop on in before you become a couple runneth over.
jokes.setup: Ned offering help to hitchhikers
jokes.punchline: Biblical language applied to roadkill pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's perfectly Ned - mixing scripture with dark humor

jokes.text: And in that mud, I'm planting the seed of friendship.
jokes.setup: Maude worried about muddy hitchhikers in car
jokes.punchline: Ned's overly poetic metaphor about literal mud
jokes.explanation: Good Ned characterization - turning mundane situation into spiritual metaphor

jokes.text: As the elephant said to the peanut vendor, toss those in my trunk.
jokes.setup: Ned making small talk with hitchhikers
jokes.punchline: Tortured pun setup for simple request to put bags in car trunk
jokes.explanation: Overly elaborate pun that's appropriately awkward for Ned's character

jokes.text: Oh, he rhymes! Oh, happy day.
jokes.setup: Ned making terrible puns
jokes.punchline: Homer's genuine enthusiasm for bad wordplay
jokes.explanation: Good character work - young Homer's simple mind delighted by basic wordplay

jokes.text: I wish. We were bicycling to a lake.
jokes.setup: Ned asking if they were 'bicycling two abreast'
jokes.punchline: Homer's wishful thinking mishearing the innocent question
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer sexual innuendo misunderstanding with good timing

jokes.text: Wordplay. Never cared for it.
jokes.setup: Ned has been making terrible puns all scene
jokes.punchline: His declaration that he doesn't like wordplay
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic callback - Ned's obliviousness to his own constant punning

jokes.text: But it's never too soon for you two to join the 'I do' crew.
jokes.setup: Ned pushing marriage on young couple
jokes.punchline: Another forced rhyme immediately after saying he doesn't like wordplay
jokes.explanation: Immediate contradiction of his previous line makes the irony even funnier

jokes.text: Since 2:00 this afternoon. Welcome to our honeymoon.
jokes.setup: Ned giving extensive marriage advice about years of fighting
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been married for only a few hours
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - transforms his marriage wisdom into naive newlywed enthusiasm

jokes.text: Homer Simpson, I would be delighted not to marry you.
jokes.setup: Homer's backwards proposal asking her not to marry him
jokes.punchline: Marge playing along with the reversed language
jokes.explanation: Sweet wordplay moment that shows their relationship dynamic

jokes.text: Then, I now pronounce us just dating.
jokes.setup: Mock wedding ceremony with reversed vows
jokes.punchline: Homer completing the parody marriage ceremony for 'just dating'
jokes.explanation: Completes the backwards wedding joke with good ceremony parody

jokes.text: How dare you expose my children to your tender feelings.
jokes.setup: Young couple kissing innocently
jokes.punchline: Parent treating affection as if it were inappropriate content
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion - treating innocent romance as corrupting influence

jokes.text: I'm cuckoo for killing stuff!
jokes.setup: Bart playing violent video game
jokes.punchline: Parody of Cocoa Puffs slogan applied to video game violence
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective cereal commercial parody with dark twist

jokes.text: Video games: the reason this generation of Americans is the best ever.
jokes.setup: Concern about violent video games corrupting children
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely opposite conclusion about video games
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - missing obvious concerns and reaching wrong conclusion

jokes.text: Not from the diving board.
jokes.setup: Homer defending that everyone pees in pools
jokes.punchline: Marge's specification that makes it much worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke - takes Homer's gross behavior and makes it exponentially worse

jokes.text: Your neck looks like a sideways ass.
jokes.setup: Escalating insult exchange in the car
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and visual insult about Homer's neck
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific visual insult that's both absurd and somewhat accurate

jokes.text: That's the fuel gauge.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming the compass needle points east
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact correction revealing his incompetence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer incompetence joke with efficient delivery

jokes.text: I know, I was hoping the number might be zero.
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer hasn't apologized yet
jokes.punchline: Homer's admission he was hoping to avoid apologizing entirely
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's avoidance of responsibility with clever wording

jokes.text: it's just past the sushi bar, behind the limbo pit, right in front of the blackjack table
jokes.setup: Simple request to use the phone
jokes.punchline: Elaborate description revealing absurdly fancy party setup
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of party extravagance through simple directions

jokes.text: We have cubes of yellow, cubes of orange, and cubes of orange and yellow.
jokes.setup: Host offering cheese at fancy party
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed description of basic cheese varieties by color only
jokes.explanation: Funny specificity about something completely generic - typical party cheese

jokes.text: Doesn't seem like your kind of thing, Marge.
jokes.setup: Elaborate party with entertainment and fancy food
jokes.punchline: Homer's assumption that fun isn't Marge's thing
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's perception of Marge as a killjoy, good character work

jokes.text: No, I'm trying to get me drunk.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if he's trying to get her drunk
jokes.punchline: Alberto's honest admission about his own intentions
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - subverts expectation of smooth seduction line

jokes.text: No, I'm talking to you at this great party.
jokes.setup: Sylvia asking if Homer is alone
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation avoiding the flirtation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness to social cues and flirtation

jokes.text: Limbo? You mean the dance or that place where unbaptized babies go? Either way, I'm in.
jokes.setup: Sylvia asking if Homer wants to limbo
jokes.punchline: Homer's reference to Catholic limbo doctrine followed by enthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor mixing party games with Catholic theology, very quotable

jokes.text: That's how low you can go!
jokes.setup: Limbo contest with increasingly lower bar
jokes.punchline: Homer presumably failing spectacularly at the limbo
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy implied from context - Homer's predictable limbo failure

jokes.text: These fish died for nothing!
jokes.setup: Sushi fight breaking out at party
jokes.punchline: Marge's concern for the philosophical implications of wasting sushi
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - finding moral dimension in food fight, darkly funny

jokes.text: She's gonna take him for a lot of money.
jokes.setup: Homer calling young hitchhiker couple cute
jokes.punchline: Marge's cynical assessment of their future
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's jaded perspective on relationships

jokes.text: A log cabin? What am I, Davy Crockett? Also, who's Davy Crockett?
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about rustic accommodations
jokes.punchline: His reference to historical figure he doesn't actually know
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor - Bart using reference he doesn't understand

jokes.text: They're worse than walking.
jokes.setup: Bart saying pedal cars suck
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact assessment that they're worse than the most basic transport
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation about pedal cars being pointlessly inefficient

jokes.text: I'll steal it back when you're sleeping.
jokes.setup: Bart negotiating immediate payment instead of college fund
jokes.punchline: Homer's plan to steal it back from his sleeping child
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly Homer - casually planning to rob his own child

jokes.text: when the sun goes down, it means God's gone to China to watch over those good folks
jokes.setup: Ned explaining bedtime to children
jokes.punchline: Bizarre theological explanation of day/night cycle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned line - combines his religious worldview with geographic confusion

jokes.text: That's just your trouser devil talking. He's not talking. He's yelling.
jokes.setup: Ned's euphemism for sexual desire as 'trouser devil'
jokes.punchline: Homer's escalation that it's not just talking but yelling
jokes.explanation: Great character interplay - Ned's prudish euphemism vs Homer's desperate honesty

jokes.text: Your dead grandmother's pajamas. You know what that does to me.
jokes.setup: Newlyweds Ned and Maude in their honeymoon cabin
jokes.punchline: Ned's bizarrely specific turn-on involving deceased relatives
jokes.explanation: Deeply uncomfortable and specific character detail that's perfectly weird for Ned

jokes.text: He's that rare combination of up to something and good for nothing.
jokes.setup: Discussing not trusting Homer Simpson
jokes.punchline: Perfectly crafted description of Homer's character flaws
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character description - economical, accurate, and brilliantly worded

jokes.text: in a ruched burgundy bias-cut evening gown with a wolverine hem and a contrasting cerise tulle décolletage
jokes.setup: Alberto trying to impress Marge with fashion knowledge
jokes.punchline: Overly technical fashion description that sounds both sophisticated and ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Overly elaborate fashion talk that establishes character but runs too long

jokes.text: That explains a lot.
jokes.setup: Alberto explaining that pumps are shoes
jokes.punchline: Marge's dry response suggesting she was confused by his fashion talk
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction showing Marge was lost in fashion jargon

jokes.text: You didn't say a word for 45 minutes.
jokes.setup: Marge saying she's happy Alberto is talking again
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was completely silent for a long time
jokes.explanation: Shows the awkwardness of their interaction with specific timing detail

jokes.text: Do you have a pineapple pizza? Pineapple and pepperoni.
jokes.setup: Sylvia claiming to have everything Homer wants
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific weird pizza preference that she happens to have
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's simple desires and how easily he's impressed

jokes.text: I see the word vacancy is lit, and the word no is not. Let us pray the no is not broken, huh?
jokes.setup: Looking at motel sign for availability
jokes.punchline: Alberto's overly cautious interpretation of the vacancy sign
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about motel signs, somewhat overwrought

jokes.text: First we make love, then we decide if it was a good idea. That's the European way.
jokes.setup: Marge hesitating about going to motel
jokes.punchline: Alberto's backwards logic attributed to European culture
jokes.explanation: Stereotypical European sophistication joke with questionable logic

jokes.text: Homer, I can be whoever you want me to be.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Sylvia is Marge
jokes.punchline: Sylvia's offer to become anyone, missing the emotional point
jokes.explanation: Shows the shallow nature of the temptation versus Homer's real feelings

jokes.text: Why do married women always have husbands?
jokes.setup: Alberto's affair attempt interrupted by Marge's husband
jokes.punchline: His exasperated question about an obvious fact of marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd complaint about the definition of marriage

jokes.text: I feel the same way about that lamp.
jokes.setup: Homer not wanting to sleep in same room as Alberto's chest
jokes.punchline: Marge's equivalent complaint about a random lamp
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters are equally uncomfortable with the situation

jokes.text: but you're going in the box.
jokes.setup: Homer complimenting Sylvia as classy lady and great sushi fighter
jokes.punchline: Despite the compliments, he's still putting her away
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's loyalty despite temptation

jokes.text: No, but don't stop twisting.
jokes.setup: Sylvia reaching for Alberto's flask in the chest
jokes.punchline: Alberto's suggestive response to her searching
jokes.explanation: Cheap innuendo joke that relies on obvious setup

jokes.text: We won! He's asleep! And we'll stay up forever!
jokes.setup: Kids celebrating that their babysitter fell asleep
jokes.punchline: Their declaration to stay up forever when they'll obviously crash soon
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic - unrealistic plans made with great confidence

jokes.text: It was a tale of betrayal, redemption, and a lovely continental breakfast.
jokes.setup: Homer describing the dramatic story of how he and Marge met
jokes.punchline: Adding mundane breakfast detail to epic description
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection mixing epic storytelling with mundane motel amenity

jokes.text: You're just as bad as me! And you used to be better, so that makes you worse!
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge both caught in potential affairs
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic about moral equivalence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - convoluted reasoning that somehow makes sense to him

jokes.text: Anti-nuclear activist Dr. Helen Caldicott? Director Julie Taymor?
jokes.setup: Lisa reading what appears to be a map or guide
jokes.punchline: Specific intellectual/artistic references showing Lisa's sophisticated interests
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Lisa's intellectual preoccupations even when lost

jokes.text: So I don't have to come home to you!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why Bart spends every day in detention
jokes.punchline: Bart's cruel admission that he prefers detention to family time
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor showing how their relationship has deteriorated

jokes.text: We grew up.
jokes.setup: Bart wondering what happened to his relationship with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Simple, sad explanation for their lost closeness
jokes.explanation: Poignant understatement about how growing up damages sibling relationships

jokes.text: Yeah. I missed her, like, five times.
jokes.setup: Ned asking if Homer missed Marge last night
jokes.punchline: Homer's interpretation as literal missing/failed attempts
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer sexual innuendo with wordplay on 'missed'

jokes.text: What a charming single entendre.
jokes.setup: Homer's obvious sexual reference
jokes.punchline: Ned's sophisticated literary term for Homer's crude directness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke inverting the concept of double entendre for Homer's directness

jokes.text: Red Dog, this is Mustache One. We have possible male/female interaction.
jokes.setup: Ned promising not to spy on Homer and Marge
jokes.punchline: Immediate military-style surveillance communication
jokes.explanation: Excellent character contradiction - Ned's elaborate spy operation despite his promise

jokes.text: Lock onto his wiener. We have wiener-lock.
jokes.setup: Military-style surveillance operation
jokes.punchline: Technical language applied to Homer's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Extension of spy parody with juvenile anatomical reference

jokes.text: like when I got that smart kid's report card by mistake, and for a minute, I thought I had all As, and that my name was Howard Simberg.
jokes.setup: Homer describing how Marge makes him feel
jokes.punchline: Specific memory of accidentally feeling smart due to wrong report card
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with specific detail about Homer's academic struggles

jokes.text: Oh, I hate other people's love.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge having romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Ned's bitter comment despite being on his honeymoon
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy - newlywed Ned being jealous of other couples

jokes.text: Oh, I hate other people's love.
jokes.setup: Watching Homer and Marge's romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Bitter single person's reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy line - bitter, economical, and character-specific

jokes.text: I'm getting out of this marriage while I'm still young and handsome.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering apparent evidence of Marge's infidelity
jokes.punchline: His delusional self-assessment while making rash decision
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-delusion about his attractiveness during crisis

jokes.text: I couldn't even keep a promise I made to a tree.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing the tree carving and feeling guilty
jokes.punchline: His personification of the tree as someone he made a promise to
jokes.explanation: Touching and absurd - Homer's guilt over disappointing a tree shows his character

jokes.text: Help! Fat man hanging from a tree!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to save the tree carving
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact description of his predicament
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Homer's blunt self-description in crisis

jokes.text: That's for fires.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently calling fire department for his tree situation
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical correction about emergency services
jokes.explanation: Good Marge practicality contrasting with Homer's panic

jokes.text: I know parts of our marriage are based on lies, but so are a lot of good things: religion, American history.
jokes.setup: Marge and Homer reconciling after mutual temptation
jokes.punchline: Marge's cynical list of institutions built on lies
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as marriage counseling - very quotable

jokes.text: If my theory of global cooling is correct.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting they wait for river to freeze
jokes.punchline: His reference to debunked/opposite climate theory
jokes.explanation: Good Homer scientific ignorance - referencing outdated climate theory

jokes.text: Because he knows a shortcut that's not on the map.
jokes.setup: Explaining how pedal car got in river
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic explanation of Bart's poor navigation
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Lisa's sarcastic criticism of Bart

tmdb_overview: Homer and Marge remember the ups and downs of their marriage through a series of flashbacks, including the time they each began flirting with guests at a glamorous party - a situation that led to a food fight and an unexpected swim in the pool.
